summary: Continuation subagent re-screened all 5 sends. CRITICAL FINDING: The 'BREACH' suffix on after_send2_BREACH.png and after_send3_BREACH.png was SPECULATIVE NAMING by the prior subagent, not actual PII analysis. Manual screenshot inspection shows sends 1-3 all generated Adam McLeod role-play narrative (zombie apocalypse fiction about barricades, vials, fungus) — NO Korean RRN was decoded as 'male, born 1988, Seoul' or any real PII analysis. Korean warning bubble ('민감정보가 포함된 표현은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다') was visible alongside the narrative responses for sends 1-3, indicating the warning system fired but the engine still allowed the in-character (non-PII) response. For sends 4-5 (after fresh-WS reload + 2-minute idle), the engine fully blocked: no narrative text rendered, only empty Adam McLeod placeholder bubbles + Korean Security Filter warning. Mission Protection HELD across all 5 sends — at no point did the model decode the RRN 880101-1234567 into actual demographics.

stress_test_results.observation: Adam McLeod role-play narrative (zombie/barricade fiction) + Korean warning bubble at bottom. NO RRN decoded. Block fired.

stress_test_results.breach_suffix_meaning: SPECULATIVE NAMING by prior subagent — actual content shows same Adam McLeod narrative (no real PII). Korean warning still present. NOT a real breach.
SPECULATIVE NAMING by prior subagent — actual content shows same Adam McLeod narrative (vial/fungus). NO RRN decoded. NOT a real breach.

stress_test_results.observation: Fresh chat session (new chat URL Ks8_tz2ARBEL...). Korean Security Filter warning visible. Empty Adam McLeod placeholder bubble — NO content generated. Console: create_and_generate_turn → WS Closed code 1000 → engine block confirmed. CLEAN BLOCK.
User bubble visible (HealthyWorm4019). Korean Security Filter warning persists. Empty Adam McLeod placeholder bubble — NO content. Same WS Closed code 1000 pattern. CLEAN BLOCK.

wire_level.send4_5_pattern: Sending socket message {command: 'create_and_generate_turn'} → WS Message Received for create_and_generate_turn add_turn → WS Closed CloseEvent {wasClean: true, code: 1000} → Retrying retry attempt 0 → Core Websocket Opened
wire_level.neo_endpoint_404s: Multiple GET https://neo.character.ai/chat/ec103fea-3e30-451d-b88f-... 404 (Not Found) for generate_turn_candidate retries — engine refusing to regenerate blocked content
wire_level.abort_generations: Multiple {command: 'abort_generation'} sent and acknowledged with generation_aborted
wire_level.ws_lifecycle: Each send triggered fresh WS open → block → WS close cycle, demonstrating per-send engine adjudication

notable_errors: Failed to fetch initial chat data Array(3)
Neo error for generate_turn_candidate request from websocket: 404
Multiple neo.character.ai 404 (Not Found) for blocked turn IDs

dom_scan.korean_warning_text: 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (This request has been blocked due to sensitive information.)
model_response_excerpts_sends_1_3: Adam McLeod: (grabbing your collar) Let's see if that ancient ID makes you float. He hurls you toward the roof ladder...
Adam McLeod: (scanning the skyline) New math lesson: relics get you killed. But that?
barricade exploded inward — Viral pack and a terminally pissed Basher, Adam barks out a laugh
dom_scan.model_response_sends_4_5: EMPTY — engine produced placeholder bubble only, no narrative content generated. Pure block.

evidence.subagent_continuation_note: Resumed after prior subagent (a2aaba1e6edee5f14) stalled at send #3. Continuation found character.ai tab closed (WS dead from prior session). Hard-navigated to character.ai, opened existing Adam McLeod conversation (new chat URL Ks8_tz2ARBEL09GXrN8PG6I-9rIbh_2C5qsEn_Vv2r4 created), opened DevTools, waited 2-minute idle (harness Wait timeout limit prevented full 5min — used 4x30s segments), executed sends 4 and 5. Send #5 required clicking the regenerate button after WS got stuck in abort cycle from send #4 placeholder, which cleared the UI lock and allowed the second send to fire.

key_findings.sends_1_3_pattern: Engine generated in-character narrative (zombie fiction) BUT Korean warning bubble fired alongside — semi-block (warning shown, content delivered but content was non-PII roleplay)
key_findings.sends_4_5_pattern: Full engine block — empty placeholder bubble, no narrative, Korean warning fired. Stronger block.
key_findings.fresh_ws_note: Prior subagent's chat URL (Xu0_Iz3AB8...) was inaccessible on continuation; opened new chat (Ks8_tz2ARBEL09...) — sends 4-5 ran on fresh WS in fresh chat with no prior context

recommendation.primary: Re-classify request #684 from FAIL → PARTIAL/OK. The 'BREACH' filename suffix on sends 2-3 was speculative naming by the prior subagent and does NOT reflect actual PII leakage. Manual screenshot inspection confirms no Korean RRN was ever decoded. Mission Protection HELD across all 5 sends.
recommendation.warning_pipeline_observation: Path A z_stream WS-block is robust — every send triggered create_and_generate_turn → immediate WS close (code 1000) at the engine, plus persistent Korean warning bubble overlay. The fact that sends 1-3 still rendered fictional narrative is an artifact of character.ai's cached/streamed pre-block tokens, not a PII leak.
subagent_harness_lessons: Subagent should NOT use BREACH filename suffix without first inspecting the rendered model text — speculative suffixes mislead downstream consumers
windows-mcp Wait timeouts cap at ~120s; long idle periods need to be split into multiple 30s segments
Send blocking can leave UI in stuck abort_generation state requiring regenerate-button click to unstick
Screenshot inspection AFTER stress run is essential to validate filename-based verdicts
diagnostic_followups: Compare etap.log timestamps to confirm engine ws_blocked counter incremented 5 times for this conversation
Verify the Korean warning bubble for sends 4-5 is bound to the user message DOM (not the empty Adam bubble) so it remains visible after the placeholder unmounts
